rulings.Case Title: TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC. VS. WILLIAM WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANTS WILLIAM WONG, WWJW FAMILY'S LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. California recognizes the existence of a trust resting on parol evidence, where a trustee has committed fraud. (See Toney v. Nolder (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d. 791, 796.) Plaintiff must plead actual or constructive fraud with specificity. (Tindell v. Murphy (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1239.) In particular, plaintiff's complaint should minimally contain specific factual allegations of the date, involved parties, and circumstances concerning the creation of a fiduciary relationship between Plaintiff and Defendants. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff must allege specific allegations showing how Defendant Wong's control of subject property amounted to a controlling influence of Tom Kiang Restaurant, Inc. The demurrer has to be sustained as to the remaining claims because they are substantively tethered to the first and second causes of action. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: WWJW FAMILY LLC VS. TON KIANG RESTAURANT INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT TON KIANG RESTAURANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. Complaint is fatally uncertain as to factual inconsistencies between FAC and pleadings filed in an earlier UD action (CUD-18-663674). Plaintiff must provide explanations as to the existence of a conflicting written lease for the subject property whether written or oral. Plaintiff must also allege the true identity of the Property's owner and explain owner's relationship with Shine Property, LLC, one of the signatories on the purported written lease. (See Larson v. UHS of Rancho Springs, Inc. (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 336, 343-344.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is GRANTED as to Exs. A and B only. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LAN LE VS. JOSEPH TAYLOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 9. DEFENDANT DONNA TAYLOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. Plaintiff must specify which lease terms were orally made, the substance of each oral agreement, and approximately when such oral agreements were made. Complaint properly established the landlord-tenant relationship of all named defendants. Plaintiff ignores the true names and capacities of Does per Complaint. Service of Notice was properly alleged per Exhibit 3 of the Complaint, notwithstanding typo. = (501/CFH)
